latin: Fremen vermibus harenae non solum non timebant, sed eis utebantur ut per desertum veherentur. Paulus tandem ipse vermem ascendere debuit. vermis ingens e harena surrexit, et Paulus uncinos in corium bestiae fixit. ventus vehemens circa eum flabat dum super harenas volabat. Fremen qui spectabant clamaverunt — Paulus vermem domuit, sicut prophetia praedixerat.

questions.prompt: How did the Fremen use the sandworms?
questions.target: They rode them to travel across the desert
choices: They rode them to travel across the desert

questions.prompt: How did Paul mount the sandworm?
questions.target: He fastened hooks into the creature's hide
choices: He fastened hooks into the creature's hide

questions.prompt: How did the Fremen react when Paul succeeded?
questions.target: They shouted — he had fulfilled the prophecy
choices: They shouted — he had fulfilled the prophecy
They challenged him to do it again

translation: The Fremen not only did not fear the sandworms, but used them to ride across the desert. Paul finally had to mount a worm himself. An enormous worm rose from the sand, and Paul fastened hooks into the beast's hide. A fierce wind blew around him as he flew over the sands. The Fremen who were watching shouted — Paul had tamed the worm, just as the prophecy had foretold.
